# Roadmap

This roadmap keeps the project focused, safe, and shippable.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundation

**Goal:** Make the project credible and contribution-ready before major coding.

### Deliverables

- GitHub organization created
- Repository initialized
- PRD added
- README added
- Code of conduct added
- Contribution guide added
- Security policy added
- Governance doc added
- Source methodology drafted
- Legal language policy drafted
- Ethics and anti-doxing policies drafted
- First 20 GitHub issues created
- First outreach message drafted
- Landing page copy drafted

### Exit criteria

- A stranger can understand the project in 5 minutes.
- A volunteer can find a safe first task.
- A reviewer can see safety boundaries.
- No sensitive submissions are requested or accepted.

---

## Phase 1 — Static Credibility MVP

**Goal:** Launch a calm, credible public information and action site without sensitive data handling.

### Core pages

- Home
- Mission
- Methodology
- Gaza accountability dossier
- Legal tracker
- Belgium action page
- EU action page
- Organization directory
- Action hub
- Press/dossier page
- About
- Correction policy

### Technical scope

- Static-first frontend
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Accessibility baseline
- Privacy-first analytics or no analytics
- No user accounts
- No witness submissions
- No sensitive media uploads
- No live scraped feeds

### Exit criteria

- Public site can be shown to contributors, reviewers, journalists, and advisors.
- Every factual claim has a source.
- Legal terminology is careful.
- Organization directory does not imply false partnerships.

---

## Phase 2 — Structured Data MVP

**Goal:** Turn public pages into structured, reusable data.

### Deliverables

- Country accountability schema
- Organization schema
- Legal case schema
- Source schema
- Action template schema
- Basic admin/editor workflow
- JSON/Markdown data files or simple CMS
- PDF dossier generation prototype

### Exit criteria

- Belgium/EU pages can be generated from structured data.
- Dossiers can be exported from approved content.
- Review workflow exists before publication.

---

## Phase 3 — Evidence Library

**Goal:** Build a curated evidence library using public, reviewed sources.

### Deliverables

- Evidence item schema
- Source grading
- Verification labels
- Search/filter interface
- Correction workflow
- Editorial review workflow
- Public methodology page linked from every evidence item

### Exclusions

Still no raw witness submission system unless expert review has occurred.

### Exit criteria

- Evidence items are clearly labeled by source and verification level.
- Social media leads are never presented as verified facts.
- Corrections can be submitted and tracked.

---

## Phase 4 — Incident Timeline and Map

**Goal:** Add geospatial and chronological context using reviewed public data.

### Deliverables

- Incident timeline
- Basic map
- Time filtering
- Incident type filtering
- Source filtering
- Verification badges
- Safety review for exact location display

### Exit criteria

- No sensitive locations are exposed.
- Exact GPS is only shown when safe, public, and necessary.
- Map performance works on mobile and lower bandwidth.

---

## Phase 5 — Partnerships and Verified Directory

**Goal:** Move from public directory to verified partnerships where possible.

### Deliverables

- Partnership policy
- Outreach templates
- Verification record format
- Partner badge rules
- Redacted confirmation storage
- Public disclaimer system

### Exit criteria

- No organization is listed as a partner without written permission.
- Directory entries are useful even without formal partnership.

---

## Phase 6 — Sensitive Submissions Research

**Goal:** Decide whether and how to support witness/source submissions safely.

### Required reviews

- human-rights documentation review;
- security review;
- privacy review;
- legal review;
- threat model;
- moderator capacity assessment;
- trauma-informed workflow review.

### Possible tools

- SecureDrop;
- GlobaLeaks;
- Uwazi/HURIDOCS-style workflow;
- external partner-managed submission channel.

### Exit criteria

- Written threat model exists.
- Written data-retention policy exists.
- Moderators are trained.
- Emergency takedown process exists.
- Decision is recorded in decision log.

---

## Phase 7 — Multi-crisis Expansion

**Goal:** Expand only after the Gaza/oPt MVP methodology is stable.

Possible future crisis modules:

- Sudan
- DRC
- Myanmar
- Tigray
- other documented mass-atrocity contexts

### Exit criteria

- Expansion does not dilute quality.
- Each crisis module has subject-matter reviewers.
- The same methodology is applied consistently.

---

## What is intentionally delayed

- user accounts;
- gamified streaks;
- leaderboards;
- public witness submissions;
- sensitive file uploads;
- automated scraping;
- donation processing;
- encrypted chat;
- Tor mirror;
- live visitor dashboard;
- AI testimony translation;
- mass-email automation.

These may be valuable later, but they are not safe first steps.
